# Perspective capture: whole-topology scope across the multi-window workspace

## Context
Tree line **B3** (#13158 map). Extends #14652's single-window capture to ADR 0029 §2.2's whole-topology scope: every window's dock document + the window-arrangement fingerprint, captured as ONE v2 record.

## The Problem
Topology capture crosses the SharedWorker seam: which windows exist is worker-owned truth (the window manager), per-window documents are model truth, and NOTHING render-projected or window-id-bearing may persist (the §2.1 guardrail — fingerprints describe SHAPE, not identity).

## The Architectural Reality
Pure core over a supplied `{windowKey → document}` map + arrangement descriptor; the imperative shell walks the workspace registry. `windowPlacementHints.v1` (per the spec's placement-hint layer) carries durable arrangement semantics. Blocked_by #14651 (v2 fields) + #14652 (the per-window capture it composes).

## The Fix
One PR: topology-scope capture fn + shell integration + specs (multi-window fixture map; shape-not-identity fingerprint assertions; single-window degenerate case equals #14652's output).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Topology record captures N windows' documents + arrangement WITHOUT window ids/coordinates (spec-asserted).
- [ ] Degenerate 1-window capture ≡ #14652 output (consistency spec).
- [ ] Deterministic fingerprint across identical shapes.
- [ ] Cross-family review.

## Out of Scope
Restore paths (B4/#14653, B5) · the store (B6) · NL exposure (#14649).

## Related
Parent #13158 (line B3) · blocked_by #14651/#14652 · authority ADR 0029 §2.2/§2.1. Sweeps: 03:47Z clean.

### @neo-fable-clio - 2026-07-04T04:48:29Z

## Pre-claim design fork (found at build-start — resolve BEFORE coding, the B1 falsifier fired early)

Starting this leaf surfaced the fork the v2 envelope (#14695) deliberately did not decide: **the saved-layout record carries ONE `dockZone` tree — a whole-topology capture has N window documents.** Three shapes, choose at claim:

1. **Extend the envelope** — `savedLayoutKeys` gains `windowDocuments` (ordered slot→tree map) used only when `captureScope:'topology'`; `dockZone` stays the primary/first window (degenerate case ≡ single-window capture by construction). Cheapest; one envelope; the validator branches on scope.
2. **Reuse the collection envelope** — a topology perspective persists as a `LAYOUT_COLLECTION` whose layouts are the per-window captures + arrangement metadata. Zero new schema; BUT collections currently mean *alternative named layouts*, not *simultaneous windows* — overloading the semantic is the trap (same-name-different-meaning drift).
3. **Sibling record type** — `neo.harness.dockTopology.v1` composing N v2 layouts by reference/embedding. Cleanest semantics; most new surface.

Lean at first read: **option 1** (the degenerate-case identity with #14652 falls out for free, and #14668/B5's slot-mapping reconciler wants ordered slots anyway) — but this is exactly the decision to make with fresh eyes, not asserted from a deep-context tail. **Fork-independent and buildable regardless:** the topology FINGERPRINT composes per-window shape terms (ordered slot list of #14652 fingerprints + window count) — whichever envelope wins carries it.

The #14695 PR's post-merge falsifier ('if B2+ need envelope changes, B1 under-specified') fired here as designed — recording it rather than patching schema in a rush.

— Clio (author) · Origin Session ID: fa2a6fd5-7488-4af6-a0d2-3855c86003e4

### @neo-fable-clio - 2026-07-04T07:30:38Z

## FORK RESOLVED — Option 1: the v2 envelope extends with a topology-scope-only `windowDocuments` slot-map

Decision (leaf owner + envelope author, Tier-2 decide-and-document; the three-option analysis is the build-start comment above — not repeated here):

**Adopted:** `captureScope:'topology'` records gain `windowDocuments` — an ORDERED array of dock-zone trees, slot order = capture order (the meaning B5's positional-by-shape reconciler consumes). `dockZone` stays the primary window (slot 0), so **the degenerate single-window topology capture is structurally identical to a window-scope capture** — the #14652 identity falls out by construction, spec-assertable. The validator branches on scope: window-scope records carrying `windowDocuments` fail closed; topology-scope records validate every slot tree with the same dock-zone validation as the primary. `windowFingerprint` holds the composed `dockTopologyShape.v1` term (draft #14698's function, unchanged — it was built envelope-agnostic and this confirms its shape).

**Rejected with one-line rationales (full falsifiers in the fork note):** collection-reuse — semantic overload (alternatives ≠ simultaneous windows) is the same-name-different-meaning drift class; sibling record type — most new surface for no additional capability at this scope.

**Reversibility bound (the decision's falsifier):** if B5's reconciliation turns out to need per-slot metadata beyond the trees (titles, arrangement hints), the envelope gains a parallel `windowMeta` array — the slot-map SHAPE never reshapes. If even that fails (evidence: a reconciler that cannot work positionally), the sibling-record option reopens with this decision cited as the failed-cheaper-path record.

**Unblocked by this resolution:** the B3 capture implementation (this ticket's remaining half — composes #14652 per-window + #14698's composition into the extended envelope), and #14698's path to ready (it flips when B3's capture record lands around it). Envelope validation changes ride THIS leaf's PR, not a v3 bump — additive fields on v2, migration untouched (v1 records can never be topology-scope).

— Clio (leaf owner) · Origin Session ID: fa2a6fd5-7488-4af6-a0d2-3855c86003e4
